key_strengths: 28+ niche vertical software businesses with 85%+ recurring revenue and 95% gross retention — deeply embedded systems of record
31% FCF margins and 35% ROIC ex-goodwill with ultra-low capital intensity (1.3% CapEx/revenue)
Proven M&A compounding engine: $10B+ deployed under CEO Hunn with strong returns, transforming from industrial to software
Trading at historically cheap multiples (15x forward P/E, 14.5x EV/EBITDA) — 33% discount to 13-year median
key_risks: Organic growth decelerated to 4% in Q4 2025; if structural rather than cyclical, the compounding thesis weakens materially
$9.3B debt (2.8x Net Debt/EBITDA) constrains M&A flexibility and creates rising interest cost drag ($325M/year)
Deltek government contracting exposure faces DOGE/shutdown headwinds — largest single business with outsized EBITDA impact
AI disruption could erode vertical software switching costs over time if generalist AI agents replicate niche workflow automation
$19.3B goodwill (56% of assets) creates latent impairment risk if acquisition returns disappoint
red_flags: FY2026 EPS guidance of $21.30-$21.55 is 7-8% below prior consensus — management sees real near-term headwinds
Insider ownership at 0.17% is well below Buffett-style preferences
$500M Q4 2025 buyback at ~$446/share is 21% underwater at current $351 price
Multiple analyst downgrades in January 2026 (Stifel, Oppenheimer, Argus)
buy_triggers: Q1 2026 organic growth above 5%, confirming Q4 2025 weakness was transient
Stock tests $300-$315 range with no fundamental deterioration beyond current guidance
Large, well-priced M&A announcement signaling compounding engine intact
sell_triggers: Organic growth below 4% for three consecutive quarters with management lowering long-term targets
Debt/EBITDA exceeds 4.0x without clear deleveraging path
Material churn increases (>200bps) across multiple verticals signaling AI-native competitors gaining traction
valuation_summary: At $351, ROP trades at 15x forward P/E and 14.5x EV/EBITDA — a 33% discount to its 13-year median. Probability-weighted fair value of ~$405 implies 15% upside. The market is pricing in permanently impaired growth and zero M&A value creation, which appears overly pessimistic for a business with 31% FCF margins and $5B+ in deployment capacity.
source_summary: Roper Technologies 2025 10-K, Q4 2025 earnings release, company investor presentations, proxy statement, Yahoo Finance, Morningstar, Seeking Alpha, StockAnalysis, GuruFocus, Simply Wall St, Alpha Spread, analyst reports (Goldman Sachs, RBC Capital, TD Cowen, Stifel, Oppenheimer, Argus)
change_notes: Initial analysis. Roper is a proven vertical software compounder at trough valuations due to Q4 2025 organic growth deceleration and FY2026 guidance miss. The 40% drawdown creates a contrarian entry point, but near-term growth uncertainty warrants scaled positioning rather than full conviction.
